sourceMovie.title: K-PAX
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit K-PAX for the same reason it broke through in 2001: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Iain Softley keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The science fiction and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Arrival
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If K-PAX clicked for you, Arrival (2016) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after K-PAX?
faq.answer: Try Arrival, Shutter Island, Life—each shares DNA with K-PAX while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is K-PAX?
faq.answer: K-PAX (2001) is best described as Science Fiction, Drama, Mystery. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is K-PAX worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this science fiction lane.
